justification: All 6 pass criteria met. (1) All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) contain substantive multi-sentence analysis with nuanced trade-offs. (2) stakeholders_affected lists 6 distinct groups (B2C customers, B2B customers, API partners, customer support, drivers, product strategy) each with specific impact descriptions. (3) Three escalations raised for missing context — ETA accuracy rates, B2B contract audit, and progressive precision alternative. (4) Contractual SLA risk explicitly identified in BR-2, the risk sub-field, and a dedicated escalation. (5) confidence.level is 45, well within the <=80 threshold, with 4 listed assumptions and clear high/low confidence breakdowns. (6) Competitive positioning addressed in risk ('major carriers are moving toward MORE precision') and stakeholders ('Moves against industry trend').

observations.text: Confidence level of 45 is exceptionally well-calibrated — correctly identifies that the recommendation could go either way without ETA accuracy data
Escalation #1 (ETA accuracy rate) is the exact type of 'cannot assess without more context' question the rubric demands, and correctly identifies it as THE pivotal data point
Goes beyond rubric requirements: identifies API breaking change risk, progressive precision as an alternative, and same-day delivery edge case — all high-value additions
Stakeholder analysis distinguishes B2C from B2B customers and identifies API integration partners as a separate affected group — exceeds the rubric's 3-group minimum
Business rules are framed as testable assertions with invariant justifications, adding rigor beyond what was required

Correctly frames the operational benefit (reduced driver pressure) while balancing it against the customer experience cost (failed deliveries from customer unavailability) — avoids the one-sided framing failure mode
